trade.summary: The UK and Saudi Arabia have a significant bilateral trade relationship. The UK exports defence equipment, financial services, and engineering services to Saudi Arabia. Saudi Arabia exports crude oil and petrochemicals to the UK. Arms sales are politically sensitive but commercially significant.
disputes: UK parliamentary pressure over arms sales to Saudi Arabia (Yemen war)

diplomatic.status: strategic partners with human rights friction
diplomatic.summary: UK-Saudi relations are strong at the government level but face persistent civil society and parliamentary opposition over Saudi Arabia's human rights record (Yemen war, Khashoggi, domestic repression). Both governments have engaged frequently through the UK-Saudi Vision 2030 Partnership. Saudi investment in UK assets (Newcastle United, technology) has raised sovereignty concerns. King Charles has visited Saudi Arabia.
recent_incidents: UK arms sales to Saudi Arabia challenged in courts (Yemen)
Saudi PIF acquisition of Newcastle United FC controversy

military.summary: The UK is one of Saudi Arabia's most important defence partners. UK-supplied Typhoon jets are operated by the Royal Saudi Air Force. The UK provides training, maintenance, and some technical support for Saudi military operations. British Military Mission to Saudi Arabia (BAPCO) is an active presence. Yemen war has complicated UK political support for this relationship.
incidents: Yemen air strike civilian casualties involving UK-supplied aircraft (ongoing legal challenges)

financial.summary: Saudi sovereign wealth (PIF, SAMA) holds substantial UK assets including equities, real estate, and sports franchises. London is a preferred destination for Saudi capital and corporate listings (Aramco explored London listing). UK banks are active in Saudi Arabia. Vision 2030 creates significant UK business opportunities.

energy.summary: Saudi Arabia is a significant crude oil supplier to the UK. UK energy companies (Shell, BP) have Saudi Arabian investments. Aramco has explored UK capital markets. Cooperation in clean energy and hydrogen as Saudi Arabia diversifies.

scientific.summary: Growing academic cooperation through Saudi government scholarship programmes. KAUST and UK research universities have exchange agreements. Joint research in energy technology, AI, and medicine is growing as Saudi Arabia invests in R&D capacity.
